methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Heven AeroTech is a Series B-stage defense and security technology company seeking valuation guidance following a $100M+ fundraise and current post-money valuation of $1.0B+. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: two public-market comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private-market companies, with particular focus on five same-stage, same-sector peers including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, applies a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x, yielding an implied value of $1.9B. Peer-median benchmarking across the five defense & security Series B comparables produces a median valuation of $1.4B, with the peer set ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B. The five-peer median reflects a balanced read on market pricing for this stage and sector.

Triangulating both methods yields a low of $1.2B, median of $1.6B, and high of $2.2B. The $400M spread between low and high reflects observable variation in peer valuations and the inherent range in stage-progression modeling. Key uncertainties include company-specific traction metrics relative to peers, capital efficiency, and near-term fundraising environment conditions for defense tech.
